changesProposed.name: DCVC

verified.name: DCVC
verified.thesis: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors. The firm invests in companies solving trillion-dollar problems at the intersection of computational approaches and AI.
verified.notes: Firm indicates '15 years active' as of sources (source [0], [1]), which would place founding around 2010-2011, but no explicit founding year stated in sources. HQ not explicitly stated in sources but database entry lists Palo Alto, CA which cannot be verified. DCVC Bio established in 2018 as a separate entity.

changes.current: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors. The firm invests in companies solving trillion-dollar problems at the intersection of computational approaches and AI. Investment areas include AI, climate, engineering, materials science, robotics, space, water, biology, and defense and security.
changes.verified: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors. The firm invests in companies solving trillion-dollar problems at the intersection of computational approaches and AI.

changesProposed.name: Engine Ventures

verified.name: Engine Ventures
verified.thesis: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage deep tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing. In 2023, the investment fund separated from The Engine nonprofit incubator.
verified.notes: Engine Ventures is the venture capital arm that spun out from The Engine nonprofit in 2023. The Engine nonprofit was founded by MIT in 2016. Sources do not provide current AUM, flagship fund name, or explicit list of current senior partners at Engine Ventures.

changesProposed.notes: Engine Ventures is the venture capital arm that spun out from The Engine nonprofit in 2023. The Engine nonprofit was founded by MIT in 2016. Sources do not provide current AUM, flagship fund name, or explicit list of current senior partners at Engine Ventures.

changesProposed.name: General Catalyst

verified.name: General Catalyst
verified.thesis: General Catalyst invests across seed, venture, and growth stages. The firm focuses on transforming critical institutions with applied AI and backing founders globally.
verified.notes: In June 2024, General Catalyst acquired La Famiglia (German VC firm), making Jeannette zu Fürstenberg a managing director. In October 2024, firm raised $8 billion in new funds, including $4.5 billion for core venture funds. Wikipedia lists Hemant Taneja as CEO & managing director and Ken Chenault as chairman & managing director. Sector focus could not be verified from explicit lists in provided sources.

changes.current: General Catalyst invests across seed, venture, and growth stages. The firm backs founders and invests in critical institutions, with focus on applied AI and global resiliency.
changes.verified: General Catalyst invests across seed, venture, and growth stages. The firm focuses on transforming critical institutions with applied AI and backing founders globally.

changesProposed.name: Point72 Ventures

verified.name: Point72 Ventures
verified.thesis: Thematic-driven investors backing ambitious founders who are building for big outcomes across critical domains. The firm brings deep sector insights, partnership, and hands-on support from seed to pre-IPO to help build enduring companies.
verified.notes: Only Steve Cohen is explicitly named as 'Chairman' on the firm's official team page (source 1). Other partners from database entry (Chris Morales, Tara Stokes, Ishan Sinha) are not found in provided sources. AUM of $400M and 'Deterrence Fund' flagship fund name cannot be verified from sources. HQ location not found in sources. Database entry references 130+ portfolio companies since 2016, which matches source language ('Since 2016, we've backed more than 130 companies').

changesProposed.name: Shield Capital

verified.name: Shield Capital
verified.thesis: Shield Capital is a venture capital firm investing in early-stage companies building technologies that matter in artificial intelligence, autonomy, cybersecurity, and space. The firm's veteran team of founders, investors, and national security leaders is mission-focused in support of entrepreneurs addressing the convergence of commercial technology and national security.
verified.notes: Sources do not verify AUM, flagship fund name, founded year, or specific HQ location. Wikipedia source [3] is about the generic term 'shield' and not relevant to the firm. SEC Form D [4] references 'SHIELD AI' which may be a different entity. Key partners extracted from firm's team page showing 'Founder and Managing Partner' and 'Partner' titles only.

changes.current: Shield Capital is a venture capital firm investing in early-stage companies building technologies that matter in artificial intelligence, autonomy, cybersecurity, and space. The firm's veteran team of founders, investors, and national security leaders focuses on the convergence of commercial technology and national security.
